role: A combined wheel visualization and control panel for type nucleation, rendering projection sectors, points, candidate blooms, and parameter pickers in one React component.
capabilities: Render registry type sectors as angular wedges
Draw membership boundary, pile horizon, and forbidden diagonal residue lines
Place and animate projection points as inside-fit, candidate-pile, or tail residue
Compute true-angle candidate blooms and born-type halos/sparks
Display nucleation report counts (born, candidates, residue)
Provide pickers for items space, types space, and rung
Provide a transparent hit layer for point selection
substance_why: The file implements a complete interactive visualization with SVG geometry, point-placement algorithms, candidate bloom logic, a local dial, report UI, and engine parameter pickers.
interactions_present: Clicking a point hit circle invokes onPick
Birth threshold range input updates the local dial
Picker selects call setNuc for space, types_space, and rung
interactions_missing: No hover tooltips or detail views for points/blooms
No loading, empty, or error states for missing projection/report data
No accessible descriptions for dynamic points or candidate zones
No reset/default controls or direct manipulation of sectors/blooms
performance_risks: AnimatePresence with motion.circle per point and layoutId can jank with many points
Unmemoized clusterTheta, sectorIndex, zoneAngle, and jitter computations run every render
Two full passes over proj.points for rendering and hit layers
SVG viewBox tied to measured size causes full re-render on resize
Framer Motion transitions applied to every point simultaneously
reuse_why: The domain-specific nucleation visualization and parameter controls are worth keeping, but they need decoupling and memoization to be maintainable.
decomposition_hints: Split SVG wheel rendering (sectors, boundaries, points, blooms) from HTML report and controls
Extract point placement math (inside/pile/tail/cornerPlace) into a pure layout hook
Separate candidate bloom computation and birth-threshold state
Move generic Picker into its own reusable component/file
Isolate hit-layer interaction handling and selection logic
